kept.text:
people are earning $30 to $150 per test cycle just by finding bugs in apps and websites from home

you just need:

- a smartphone
- stable internet
- a paypal or upwork account
- that's literally it

https://t.co/P680EuhzFO pays you to test real apps and websites for companies like microsoft, spotify, and facebook

open worldwide, free to join, no experience needed

here's how it works:

1. sign up
> go to https://t.co/Ts0vhldvxR
> create your account and confirm your email

2. complete your profile
> add your full name, country, and availability
> add every device you own (phone, tablet, laptop)
> more devices = more test invites

3. pass the assessment
> ~15 to 30 minute QA skills and english test
> study the notions page before starting
> you get 2 attempts so don't rush it

4. wait for approval
> takes 1 to 3 working days
> once approved you start receiving invites by email

5. accept invites and start testing
> respond fast, spots fill quickly
> follow instructions carefully
> submit detailed bug reports with screenshots and steps to reproduce

6. get paid
> payments processed 12 to 14 days after each cycle
> minimum payout: $5 (unused earnings roll over)
> paid via paypal or upwork

one thing most people don't know tho

the better your bug reports are the higher your rank goes

higher rank = up to 2x payout multiplier

quality always beats speed here

bookmark this and tag someone who needs a side income

kept.text:
$PYPL One of the things that former CEO Alex Chriss did was integrate the siloed aspects of PayPal's business into a whole unit.

This was necessary. PayPal had failed to digest and accelerate past acquisitions, to the extent that Pay With Venmo competed directly with branded paypal (at a lower take rate). 

The only way this move makes sense is selling Venmo, which is easier to do as an independent business unit, which is what I am expecting this indicates.

kept.text:
US influencer after China tour: ‘E-wallets! Bullet trains! Food carts on demand! This is the future!’

Reality check:

• PayPal dropped in 1998 — digital payments 25+ years ago.
• Grubhub was delivering food apps in 2004.
• Japan ran bullet trains since 1964, France TGV since 1981.

China scaled QR-code everything and HSR massively by leapfrogging old systems. Impressive rollout.

But calling it revolutionary ‘new tech’ ignores why the West didn’t go all-in earlier: mature credit cards with rewards, legacy banking that already worked, car-centric infrastructure, privacy regs, and fragmented markets. No need to burn the old when it functioned fine.

Hype is easy. Historical context is better.

kept.text:
$SOFI

This earnings was a C+.

Q1 is never the strongest earnings, and the lending growth was phenomenal.

The tech platform however has literally moved from the AWS of Fintech to something comparable of PayPal.

Huge Invest account growth.
Barely any Credit Card additions.
Good deposit growth.
No guidance increase.
Incredible Student & Home loan growth.
Fee based revenue flat. 

Strange numbers, looking to see what Noto’s commentary is.
